name: FAO Constitution 1945 (Food and Agriculture Organization)
summary: The Constitution of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO Constitution) is a global supranational meta-framework foundational-FAO-treaty framework adopted at the Quebec Conference — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-food-agriculture-and-nutrition framework + first UN specialized agency founded framework (FAO Constitution adopted 16 October 1945 in Quebec City Canada by 42 founding states + effective 16 October 1945 — formal foundational FAO treaty framework establishing comprehensive FAO organisation framework, distinct from rest-of-modern-international-treaty frameworks which operate on different substantive areas, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-food-agriculture-and-nutrition framework + first UN specialized agency founded framework + 16 October World Food Day commemoration framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Codex Alimentarius food-standards framework via FAO-WHO-joint-1963 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Convention on Plant Protection framework via 1951 IPPC framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Right-to-Food framework via Article 11 ICESCR partnership framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 2030 Zero Hunger SDG 2 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Gaza-2023-2024-food-insecurity-IPC-Phase-5-famine framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Sudan-civil-war-2023-largest-food-insecurity-crisis-globally framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Black Sea Grain Initiative 2022-2023 framework via FAO + UN-mediated framework via Russia + Ukraine grain-corridor framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Codex Alimentarius framework (Codex Alimentarius Commission established 1963 by FAO + WHO joint framework providing formal international-food-standards framework — formal foundational Codex Alimentarius framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal international-food-standards framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal FAO-WHO-joint framework), as the central jurisdiction of the formal IPPC International Plant Protection Convention framework (IPPC International Plant Protection Convention adopted 6 December 1951 in Rome Italy + effective 3 April 1952 + revised 1979 + revised 1997 providing formal international-plant-protection framework — formal foundational IPPC framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal international-plant-protection framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal FAO IPC-Integrated-Food-Security-Phase-Classification framework (FAO IPC Integrated Food Security Phase Classification framework launched 2004 providing formal five-phase food-security classification framework with IPC Phase 5 famine framework — formal foundational FAO IPC framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal five-phase food-security classification framework with IPC Phase 5 famine framework). The FAO Constitution is binding upon 195 FAO member states + 2 associate members + 1 EU framework as of 2026 — comprehensive universal coverage framework. The Constitution is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal FAO Right-to-Food framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal family-food-security framework + UNICEF-FAO-joint child-nutrition framework. The FAO Conference + FAO Council + FAO Director-General + FAO Secretariat are the formal FAO bodies; national-level apex courts operate as judicial bodies. The FAO Constitution is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.title: FAO Constitution
statutory_framework.relevance: Constitution of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations adopted 16 October 1945 in Quebec City Canada + effective 16 October 1945 — formal foundational FAO treaty framework establishing comprehensive FAO organisation framework.

statutory_framework.title: IPPC
statutory_framework.relevance: International Plant Protection Convention adopted 6 December 1951 in Rome Italy + effective 3 April 1952 + revised 1979 + revised 1997 — formal foundational IPPC international-plant-protection framework.

statutory_framework.title: Codex Alimentarius Commission
statutory_framework.relevance: Codex Alimentarius Commission established 1963 by FAO + WHO joint framework — formal foundational Codex Alimentarius international-food-standards framework.

statutory_framework.citation: FAO IPC Integrated Food Security Phase Classification 2004
statutory_framework.title: FAO IPC
statutory_framework.relevance: FAO IPC Integrated Food Security Phase Classification framework launched 2004 — formal foundational FAO IPC five-phase food-security classification framework.

statutory_framework.citation: FAO Voluntary Guidelines on the Right to Adequate Food 2004
statutory_framework.title: FAO Voluntary Guidelines on the Right to Adequate Food
statutory_framework.relevance: FAO Voluntary Guidelines on the Right to Adequate Food in the Context of National Food Security adopted 24 November 2004 — formal foundational FAO Right-to-Food framework.

apex_courts.name: FAO Conference
apex_courts.role: Formal supreme decision-making body for FAO.

apex_courts.name: FAO Director-General
apex_courts.role: Formal administrative head body for FAO.

anonymisation_convention: FAO-related communications operate via state-state framework via FAO Conference + Council; individual communications not formally accepted under FAO framework.

key_developments.title: Hot Springs Conference
key_developments.description: United Nations Conference on Food and Agriculture Hot Springs Conference held 18 May - 3 June 1943 in Hot Springs Virginia USA — predecessor framework to FAO.

key_developments.title: FAO Constitution adopted + FAO founded
key_developments.description: FAO Constitution adopted 16 October 1945 in Quebec City Canada by 42 founding states + effective 16 October 1945 + FAO founded 16 October 1945 — formal foundational FAO treaty framework + first UN specialized agency operational.

key_developments.title: IPPC International Plant Protection Convention adopted + FAO Headquarters Rome
key_developments.description: IPPC International Plant Protection Convention adopted 6 December 1951 in Rome Italy + FAO Headquarters moved from Washington DC to Rome Italy 1951 — formal IPPC + FAO Rome Headquarters framework operational.

key_developments.title: Codex Alimentarius Commission established
key_developments.description: Codex Alimentarius Commission established 1963 by FAO + WHO joint framework — formal foundational Codex Alimentarius international-food-standards framework operational.

key_developments.title: World Food Day established + IPPC revised
key_developments.description: World Food Day established 1979 at 20th FAO Conference Rome Italy + commemorated 16 October FAO founding date + IPPC revised 1979 — formal World Food Day + IPPC revised framework operational.

key_developments.title: World Food Summit + Rome Declaration
key_developments.description: World Food Summit held 13-17 November 1996 in Rome Italy + Rome Declaration on World Food Security adopted 13 November 1996 — formal foundational FAO World Food Summit framework.

key_developments.title: FAO Voluntary Guidelines on Right to Food + IPC launched
key_developments.description: FAO Voluntary Guidelines on the Right to Adequate Food in the Context of National Food Security adopted 24 November 2004 + FAO IPC Integrated Food Security Phase Classification framework launched 2004 — formal foundational FAO Right-to-Food + IPC framework operational.

key_developments.title: Sustainable Development Goals adopted including SDG 2 Zero Hunger
key_developments.description: Sustainable Development Goals SDGs adopted 25 September 2015 including SDG 2 Zero Hunger framework — formal foundational FAO SDG 2 Zero Hunger framework operational.

key_developments.title: Black Sea Grain Initiative + Russia invades Ukraine
key_developments.description: Russia invaded Ukraine 24 February 2022 + Black Sea Grain Initiative signed 22 July 2022 in Istanbul Turkey by Russia + Ukraine + Turkey + UN-mediated + FAO-involved + Russia withdrew 17 July 2023 — formal FAO + UN-mediated Black Sea Grain Initiative framework operational.

key_developments.title: Gaza famine IPC Phase 5
key_developments.description: Gaza famine IPC Phase 5 declared March 2024 + IPC Phase 4-5 famine framework operational in Gaza 2023-2024 framework — formal Gaza famine IPC framework operational.

structural_findings: FAO Constitution operates a supranational global binding-treaty framework — places FAO Constitution in the global supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-food-agriculture-and-nutrition framework + first UN specialized agency founded framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-food-agriculture-and-nutrition framework + first UN specialized agency founded framework + 16 October World Food Day commemoration framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Codex Alimentarius food-standards framework via FAO-WHO-joint-1963 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Convention on Plant Protection framework via 1951 IPPC framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Right-to-Food framework via Article 11 ICESCR partnership framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal 2030 Zero Hunger SDG 2 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Gaza-2023-2024-food-insecurity-IPC-Phase-5-famine framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Sudan-civil-war-2023-largest-food-insecurity-crisis-globally framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Black Sea Grain Initiative 2022-2023 framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal Codex Alimentarius framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal international-food-standards framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal FAO-WHO-joint framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal IPPC International Plant Protection Convention framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal international-plant-protection framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal FAO IPC-Integrated-Food-Security-Phase-Classification framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal five-phase food-security classification framework with IPC Phase 5 famine framework.
195 FAO member state + 2 associate member + 1 EU ratification framework is structurally distinctive globally — comprehensive universal coverage framework.

1943 Hot Springs + 1945 FAO Constitution + 1951 IPPC + Rome HQ + 1963 Codex + 1979 World Food Day + IPPC revised + 1996 World Food Summit + 2004 Right to Food + IPC + 2015 SDG 2 + 2022 Russia-Ukraine + Black Sea Grain Initiative + 2024 Gaza famine FAO-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

FAO Constitution + Codex Alimentarius + IPPC + Right to Food Voluntary Guidelines + IPC + Black Sea Grain Initiative formal sextet framework with limited PA-intersection framework via formal family-food-security framework + UNICEF-FAO-joint child-nutrition framework — distinctive in operating limited PA-intersection framework via family-food-security framework.
editorial_notes: FAO Constitution jurisdiction sidecar — supranational global international-food-agriculture-and-nutrition foundational-FAO-treaty meta-framework (FAO Constitution 1945 Quebec + IPPC 1951/1952 + Codex Alimentarius 1963 FAO-WHO-joint + IPC 2004 + Right to Food Voluntary Guidelines 2004 + 1943 Hot Springs predecessor + 1951 Rome HQ + 1979 World Food Day + 1996 World Food Summit + 2015 SDG 2 + 2022 Black Sea Grain Initiative + 2024 Gaza famine). Only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating on formal international-food-agriculture-and-nutrition framework + first UN specialized agency founded framework + 16 October World Food Day commemoration framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Codex Alimentarius food-standards framework via FAO-WHO-joint-1963 framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IPPC framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Right-to-Food framework via Article 11 ICESCR partnership + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal IPC five-phase food-security classification framework + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Gaza famine IPC Phase 5 + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Sudan-civil-war-2023-largest-food-insecurity-crisis-globally + only modern global supranational binding-treaty framework operating formal Black Sea Grain Initiative 2022-2023 + central jurisdiction of formal Codex Alimentarius + central jurisdiction of formal IPPC + central jurisdiction of formal IPC five-phase food-security with IPC Phase 5 famine + 195 FAO member states + 2 associate members + 1 EU + FAO Headquartered Rome since 1951 + 1943 Hot Springs + 1945 FAO Constitution + 1951 IPPC + Rome HQ + 1963 Codex + 1979 World Food Day + 1996 World Food Summit + 2004 Right to Food + IPC + 2015 SDG 2 + 2022 Russia-Ukraine + Black Sea Grain Initiative + 2024 Gaza famine FAO-framework-development sequence.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator. NOTE: FAO Right-to-Food framework operates limited intersection with parental-alienation framework via formal family-food-security framework + UNICEF-FAO-joint child-nutrition framework.

Joins global + supranational + international-food-agriculture-and-nutrition-foundational-FAO-treaty cluster + FAO-Constitution-only-global-supranational-binding-treaty-on-food-agriculture-globally-distinctive + first-UN-specialized-agency-founded + 16-October-World-Food-Day + Codex-Alimentarius-FAO-WHO-joint-1963 + IPPC-International-Plant-Protection-Convention-1951-Rome + IPC-Integrated-Food-Security-Phase-Classification-2004-five-phase-Phase-5-famine + Right-to-Food-Voluntary-Guidelines-2004-Article-11-ICESCR-partnership + SDG-2-Zero-Hunger + Gaza-famine-IPC-Phase-5-2024 + Sudan-civil-war-2023-largest-food-insecurity-globally + Black-Sea-Grain-Initiative-2022-2023-Istanbul-Russia-withdrew + 195-FAO-member-states + 2-associate-members + 1-EU + FAO-Headquartered-Rome-since-1951 + 1943-Hot-Springs-Conference-predecessor + 1996-World-Food-Summit-Rome-Declaration + family-food-security-PA-intersection-via-UNICEF-FAO-joint clusters within the corpus.
